title: Add entity observation events to MineflayerAdapter
description:
Add periodic entity scanning to MineflayerAdapter so the agent knows what mobs/animals/players are nearby. This is the first step in closing the mob/entity awareness gap documented in `Data/Pages/Audit/memorysmith_situational_awareness_design_doc_20260625T020914Z.md`.

Implementation:
1. Add a periodic scan (every 2-3 seconds) using `bot.entities` to collect nearby living entities within a configurable radius (default 32 blocks).
2. Filter to living entities: mobs (hostile/passive), animals, other players. Exclude item drops, paintings, minecarts, etc.
3. Emit `entityObserved` events with: entity type, display name or mob type, position (x/y/z floored), distance from bot, and health if available.
4. Track which entities were seen last scan; emit `entityDeparted` when an entity leaves the radius.
5. Use tunable constants at the top of index.js:
   - `ENTITY_SCAN_RADIUS = 32` (blocks)
   - `ENTITY_SCAN_INTERVAL_MS = 2000` (ms between scans)
6. Do NOT scan every tick — use `setInterval` or tick-count throttling.

Acceptance:
- Running agent logs `entityObserved` events for nearby cows, zombies, etc.
- Entities that leave the radius emit `entityDeparted`.
- No entities reported when none are nearby.
- Item drops are NOT included (those are handled by playerCollect).

Refs: `memorysmith_situational_awareness_design_doc_20260625T020914Z.md` Phase 1

comments.author: anonymous
comments.body:
**Linked to external audit (2026-06-29):** The audit confirmed that entity observation (`scanNearbyEntities`) detects threats but the adapter has no attack/flee action to act on them (TSK-0258). The `physicsTick` hook is currently commented out pending chat interference testing (noted in Sprint 55 handoff). The audit recommends closing this loop with combat/retreat actions before entity observation is fully useful.

See: `Data/Pages/Audits/mineflayer-audit-claude-06-29-26.md` and `Data/Pages/council/mineflayer-external-audit-council-2026-06-29.md`
